vineyards.name: Maison Trimbach
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Geisberg, Osterberg, Rosacker)
vineyards.winemaker: Pierre Trimbach (cellar) with Anne and Julien Trimbach
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and seated tastings by appointment at the Ribeauville maison; reservations through the family website.
vineyards.description: Thirteen-generation Ribeauville house founded in 1626, source of Clos Sainte-Hune Riesling from the Rosacker Grand Cru and a long-standing reference for bone-dry Alsace Riesling.
vineyards.tip: Tastings are by appointment; allow time to walk up to the Rosacker hill above Hunawihr where Clos Sainte-Hune sits.

vineyards.name: Famille Hugel
vineyards.address: 3 Rue de la Premiere Armee, 68340 Riquewihr, France
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Schoenenbourg, Sporen) and Vendanges Tardives / Selection de Grains Nobles
vineyards.owner: Hugel family (Marc, Marc-Andre and Jean-Frederic Hugel)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tours and seated tastings at the Riquewihr house; appointments preferred.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1639, the 13th-generation Riquewihr house that led the 1984 reform giving Alsace its Vendanges Tardives and Selection de Grains Nobles laws.
vineyards.tip: The Hugel Schoenenbourg Riesling sits below the family's cellars; the village walking route passes the foot of the Grand Cru.

verified.address_quoted: 3, rue de la premiere Armee, 68340 Riquewihr

vineyards.name: Leon Beyer
vineyards.address: 2 Rue de la Premiere Armee, 68420 Eguisheim, France
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC (dry-style focus)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings by appointment at the Eguisheim house, central to the village ring.
vineyards.description: Eguisheim winegrowers since 1580, the house that built the dry-style reputation of Alsace Gewurztraminer and Riesling and supplies many French three-star tables.

verified.address_quoted: 2, rue de la premiere armee, 68420 Eguisheim

vineyards.name: Domaines Schlumberger
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Kitterle, Kessler, Saering, Spiegel)
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Guebwiller estate; bookings via the website.
vineyards.description: Guebwiller estate spanning 130 hectares including 70 hectares of Grand Cru terraces on Kitterle, Kessler, Saering and Spiegel, a substantial Grand Cru holding under single ownership.
vineyards.tip: The Kitterle terraces above the cellar climb at angles up to 50 percent; the cellar visit walks part of the slope before tasting.

vineyards.name: Domaine Weinbach
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Schlossberg, Furstentum, Mambourg)
vineyards.owner: Faller family (Catherine, Eddy and Theo Faller)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings by appointment at the Clos des Capucins; cellar visits run through the year.
vineyards.description: The Faller family's Clos des Capucins at the foot of Schlossberg in Kaysersberg, biodynamic since the 2000s and one of the touchstones for Alsace Riesling and Pinot Gris.
vineyards.tip: The walled Clos des Capucins surrounds the cellar; visits typically include a walk through the clos before tasting.

verified.address_quoted: Clos des Capucins, 25 route du Vin - 68240 Kaysersberg

vineyards.name: Domaine Zind-Humbrecht
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Brand, Hengst, Goldert, Rangen) and Selection de Grains Nobles
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Turckheim winery; reservations via the website.
vineyards.description: Turckheim estate built from the 1959 union of the Zind and Humbrecht families and run by Olivier Humbrecht MW, holding parcels in Brand, Hengst and the volcanic Clos Saint-Urbain on the Rangen.
vineyards.tip: Clos Saint-Urbain sits inside the Rangen amphitheatre of Thann; tastings at the Turckheim cellar walk through every Grand Cru in succession.

vineyards.name: Domaine Marcel Deiss
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Altenberg de Bergheim, Schoenenbourg, Mambourg) and complantation field-blends
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Bergheim domaine; bookings via the website.
vineyards.description: Bergheim estate that revived the Alsace climat tradition with co-planted Grand Cru field-blends, biodynamic since 1998 and Demeter certified, run by Mathieu Deiss with his father Jean-Michel.
vineyards.tip: Deiss bottles many of his Grands Crus as field-blends rather than single varietals; the Altenberg de Bergheim cuvee is a thirteen-variety complantation.

vineyards.name: Domaine Albert Boxler
vineyards.address: 78 Rue des Trois Epis, 68230 Niedermorschwihr, France
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Sommerberg, Brand)
vineyards.owner: Boxler family (Jean Boxler and son Jean-Marie)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tastings strictly by appointment; the estate is small and allocation-driven.
vineyards.description: Single-village Niedermorschwihr estate run by Jean Boxler and his son Jean-Marie, with thirteen hectares almost entirely on the granite Grands Crus Sommerberg and Brand. Allocation only.
vineyards.tip: The Sommerberg climbs straight above the village; book ahead and walk up before tasting.

vineyards.name: Domaine Josmeyer
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Hengst, Brand)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment; the modern domaine sits in central Wintzenheim.
vineyards.description: Wintzenheim domaine farmed biodynamically across 28 hectares including Grand Cru Hengst and Brand, run by sisters Celine and Isabelle Meyer.

vineyards.name: Domaine Albert Mann
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Hengst, Schlossberg, Furstentum, Pfersigberg, Steingrubler, Mambourg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Wettolsheim winery.
vineyards.description: Wettolsheim domaine run by brothers Maurice and Jacky Barthelme, biodynamic since 1997 with parcels in six Grands Crus across the Colmar belt and Kaysersberg amphitheatre.

vineyards.name: Domaine Bott-Geyl
vineyards.address: 1 Rue du Petit Chateau, 68980 Beblenheim, France
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Sonnenglanz, Schoenenbourg, Sporen, Mandelberg, Furstentum, Schlossberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Beblenheim domaine; appointments preferred.
vineyards.description: Beblenheim family domaine with parcels in six Grands Crus, converted to organic in 2000 and certified Demeter biodynamic, run by Jean-Christophe Bott.

vineyards.name: Domaine Ostertag
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Muenchberg) plus lieux-dits Fronholz, Zellberg, Heissenberg
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits by appointment at the Epfig domaine.
vineyards.description: Epfig domaine biodynamic since 1996 and Demeter certified, founded by Andre Ostertag and now run with his son Arthur across 88 plots on schist, sandstone and granite.

vineyards.name: Domaine Mure - Clos Saint-Landelin
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Vorbourg, including monopole Clos Saint-Landelin)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Clos Saint-Landelin cellar on the Vorbourg.
vineyards.description: Rouffach domaine owning the monopole Clos Saint-Landelin on the Vorbourg Grand Cru, biodynamic since the 1990s and one of the strongest Alsace Pinot Noir addresses.
vineyards.tip: Clos Saint-Landelin is a 15-hectare south-facing limestone slope at the southern end of Vorbourg; the tasting room sits at the foot of the clos.

vineyards.name: Domaine Schoffit
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Rangen de Thann including monopole Clos Saint-Theobald, Sommerberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Colmar winery.
vineyards.description: Colmar family estate with the rare Clos Saint-Theobald monopole on the volcanic Rangen de Thann, plus a small Sommerberg holding and the broad Harth lieu-dit at Colmar.

vineyards.name: Domaine Dirler-Cade
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Spiegel, Saering, Kessler, Kitterle)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Bergholtz winery.
vineyards.description: Bergholtz family domaine biodynamic since 1998 with parcels in four Guebwiller Grands Crus and the only producer alongside Schlumberger holding all four cru in significant quantity.

vineyards.name: Domaine Pierre Frick
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Steinert, Vorbourg, Eichberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits by appointment; the domaine focuses on natural-style bottlings.
vineyards.description: Pfaffenheim natural-wine producer, Demeter certified since 1981 and an early Alsace biodynamic adopter. Many cuvees released without added sulphur.

vineyards.name: Domaine Christian Binner
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Schlossberg, Kaefferkopf)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Ammerschwihr winery.
vineyards.description: Ammerschwihr family domaine since 1770, a recognised Alsace natural-wine address with low-to-zero sulphur cuvees and a strong skin-contact and orange-style line.

vineyards.name: Domaine Barmes-Buecher
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Hengst, Steingrubler, Pfersigberg)
vineyards.owner: Barmes-Buecher family (Sophie, Genevieve and Maxime Barmes)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Wettolsheim winery.
vineyards.description: Wettolsheim biodynamic estate certified since 2001, with parcels on Hengst, Steingrubler and Pfersigberg, member of Biodyvin and Renaissance des Appellations.

vineyards.name: Domaine Marc Tempe
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Mambourg, Schoenenbourg, Furstentum, Zinnkoepfle)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits by appointment in Zellenberg.
vineyards.description: Zellenberg micro-domaine biodynamic since 1996, run by Marc Tempe with extended elevage in foudre and old Burgundy barrels and a focus on Grand Cru terroir transparency.

vineyards.name: Kuentz-Bas
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Eichberg, Pfersigberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Husseren-les-Chateaux winery on the highest village of the Route des Vins.
vineyards.description: Husseren-les-Chateaux house founded in 1795, certified organic since 2007 and biodynamic since 2008, sitting on the highest village of the Route des Vins.

vineyards.name: Domaine Valentin Zusslin
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Pfingstberg) and Cremant d'Alsace
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Orschwihr winery.
vineyards.description: Orschwihr family domaine since 1691, biodynamic since 1997 and Demeter certified, run by 13th-generation siblings Marie and Jean-Paul Zusslin with Pfingstberg Grand Cru holdings.

vineyards.name: Domaine Bohn
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC (slate terroir of Reichsfeld)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits by appointment at the Reichsfeld estate.
vineyards.description: Reichsfeld father-and-son estate on a rare slate-and-volcanic terroir at altitude in the Bas-Rhin, organic certified since 2020 and farming without ploughing.

vineyards.name: Domaine Leon Boesch
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Zinnkoepfle)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the bio-climatic stone-and-straw cellar in Westhalten.
vineyards.description: Westhalten family domaine since 1640 in the dry Vallee Noble, Demeter certified biodynamic since 2000 with parcels on the steep Zinnkoepfle Grand Cru.

vineyards.name: Domaine Rolly Gassmann
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC (Rorschwihr lieux-dits) and Selection de Grains Nobles
vineyards.owner: Rolly Gassmann family (Pierre and Marie-Therese Gassmann)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at the Rorschwihr winery.
vineyards.description: Rorschwihr family domaine with library cellar stretching back four decades, specialising in long-aged Rorschwihr lieux-dits Riesling, Gewurztraminer and Pinot Gris.
vineyards.tip: Rolly Gassmann releases its wines with extended bottle age; current vintages frequently sit five to ten years behind release.

vineyards.name: Cave de Ribeauville
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Geisberg, Osterberg, Kirchberg de Ribeauville, Rosacker, Altenberg de Bergheim) and Cremant d'Alsace
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and free tastings at the boutique on Route de Colmar.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1895, an early French wine cooperative pooling Riesling Grand Cru parcels from forty Ribeauville growers across Geisberg, Osterberg and Kirchberg.

vineyards.name: Wolfberger
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Eichberg, Pfersigberg, Hatschbourg, Hengst) and Cremant d'Alsace
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits at the Eguisheim boutique and the eaux-de-vie distillery on site.
vineyards.description: Eguisheim cooperative founded in 1902, pooling 1200 hectares across 480 grower families. Major source of Cremant d'Alsace at volume and a substantial regional eaux-de-vie distiller.

vineyards.name: Maison Pierre Sparr Successeurs
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Mambourg, Schlossberg, Brand) and Cremant d'Alsace
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Beblenheim shop.
vineyards.description: Historic 1680 Sigolsheim house now headquartered in Beblenheim, owned since 2018 by the Wolfberger cooperative and a steady source of Mambourg Gewurztraminer.

vineyards.name: Maison Lucien Albrecht
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Pfingstberg) and Cremant d'Alsace
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits at the Orschwihr house; Cremant tastings are a particular focus.
vineyards.description: Orschwihr Cremant specialist founded by Balthazar Albrecht in 1698, a historic house involved in the 1976 founding of the Cremant d'Alsace AOC.

vineyards.name: Domaine Trapet Alsace
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Schoenenbourg, Sporen)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits by appointment in Riquewihr; allocation-only releases.
vineyards.description: The Burgundian Trapet family's Alsace satellite at Riquewihr, biodynamic and run by Andree Trapet alongside the Gevrey-Chambertin domaine, with parcels on Schoenenbourg and Sporen.

vineyards.name: Domaine Sipp Mack
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Rosacker, Osterberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Hunawihr winery; appointments preferred.
vineyards.description: Hunawihr family domaine with parcels on the Rosacker and Osterberg Grands Crus, certified organic with a consistent mid-priced Riesling line.

vineyards.name: Cave de Turckheim
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Brand, Hengst, Sommerberg, Wineck-Schlossberg) and Cremant d'Alsace
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Turckheim cooperative shop.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1955, a Haut-Rhin cooperative pooling 400 hectares across Turckheim, Niedermorschwihr and Katzenthal with all four local Grands Crus represented.

vineyards.name: Bestheim
vineyards.address: 3 Rue du General de Gaulle, 68630 Bennwihr, France
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Marckrain, Mambourg, Furstentum, Sonnenglanz) and Cremant d'Alsace
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits at the Bennwihr boutique; group visits at the Westhalten Cremant cellar.
vineyards.description: Bennwihr-based cooperative born after the wartime destruction of the village, today a major Alsace Cremant producer with two cellars and 1300 hectares under contract.

verified.address_quoted: 3 rue du General de Gaulle, 68630 Bennwihr

vineyards.name: Cave de Beblenheim
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Sonnenglanz, Mandelberg, Furstentum, Schoenenbourg) and Cremant d'Alsace
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Beblenheim boutique; the 15th-century house holds a 1836 wine press.
vineyards.description: Beblenheim cooperative founded in 1952, pooling 285 hectares from 120 growers across Sonnenglanz, Mandelberg and Schoenenbourg Grands Crus.

vineyards.name: Cave des Vignerons de Pfaffenheim
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Steinert, Goldert, Hatschbourg, Zinnkoepfle) and Cremant d'Alsace
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Pfaffenheim cooperative boutique.
vineyards.description: Pfaffenheim cooperative founded in 1957, marketed under the Pfaff label and a steady source of Steinert, Goldert and Hatschbourg Grand Cru cuvees.

vineyards.name: Domaine Sylvie Spielmann
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Altenberg de Bergheim, Kanzlerberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits by appointment at the Bergheim domaine.
vineyards.description: Bergheim single-vigneronne domaine on the family's former gypsum quarry, biodynamic since 1999 with monopole-style holdings on the Kanzlerberg Grand Cru.

vineyards.name: Domaine Schoenheitz
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC (Munster valley slopes, lieu-dit Holder, Herrenreben, Linsenberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Wihr-au-Val winery in the Munster valley.
vineyards.description: Munster valley domaine planted by Henri and Dominique Schoenheitz on the steep slopes above Wihr-au-Val, an outpost of viticulture at altitude in the Vosges foothills.

vineyards.name: Domaine Ernest Burn - Clos Saint-Imer
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Goldert, with monopole Clos Saint-Imer)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits by appointment at the Gueberschwihr winery.
vineyards.description: Gueberschwihr family domaine owning the Clos Saint-Imer monopole on the Goldert Grand Cru, a notable address for Muscat in southern Alsace.

vineyards.name: Domaine Bott Freres
vineyards.address: 13 Avenue du General de Gaulle, 68150 Ribeauville, France
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Geisberg, Osterberg, Rosacker)
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Ribeauville winery; open Sundays in season.
vineyards.description: Ribeauville house founded in 1835, a veteran village family domaine with parcels on the three local Grands Crus and a substantial Cremant programme.

verified.address_quoted: 13 Avenue du General de Gaulle, 68150 Ribeauville

vineyards.name: Domaine Henry Fuchs
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Kirchberg de Ribeauville)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings by appointment at the Ribeauville winery.
vineyards.description: Ribeauville family domaine certified organic, working terroirs across the village including the Kirchberg de Ribeauville Grand Cru.

vineyards.name: Domaine Paul Blanck et Fils
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Furstentum, Mambourg, Schlossberg, Wineck-Schlossberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Kientzheim winery.
vineyards.description: Kientzheim domaine farmed by cousins Philippe and Frederic Blanck across 36 hectares with parcels in four neighbouring Grands Crus on granite and limestone.

vineyards.name: Domaine Albert Seltz
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Zotzenberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings by appointment at the 16th-century Mittelbergheim cellar; guided tours at 10:45 and 16:30.
vineyards.description: Fourteen-generation Mittelbergheim domaine, a long-standing advocate for Sylvaner's place at Grand Cru level and producer of well-regarded Zotzenberg cuvees.

vineyards.name: Domaine Marc Kreydenweiss
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Wiebelsberg, Moenchberg, Kastelberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Andlau winery; appointments preferred.
vineyards.description: Andlau biodynamic pioneer farming since 1989, three Bas-Rhin Grands Crus on schist, sandstone and limestone, now run by Marc's son Antoine.

vineyards.name: Domaine Rieflé-Landmann
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Steinert, Vorbourg, Zinnkoepfle, Bollenberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Pfaffenheim winery.
vineyards.description: Pfaffenheim family domaine, merged with the Landmann estate of Soultzmatt, with holdings spanning Steinert, Vorbourg, Zinnkoepfle and the Bollenberg lieu-dit.

vineyards.name: Maison Gustave Lorentz
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Altenberg de Bergheim, Kanzlerberg) and Cremant d'Alsace
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Bergheim winery; tours run daily in season.
vineyards.description: Bergheim house since 1836, certified organic across its 33 estate hectares with Altenberg de Bergheim and Kanzlerberg Grand Cru bottlings at the top of the range.

vineyards.name: Domaine Wunsch et Mann
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Hengst, Steingrubler, Pfersigberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Wettolsheim winery; appointments preferred.
vineyards.description: Wettolsheim family domaine certified organic, with parcels on three Grands Crus around the Colmar belt and a strong Cremant programme.

vineyards.name: Domaine Jean-Francois Ginglinger
vineyards.address: 8 Place Charles de Gaulle, 68420 Eguisheim, France
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Pfersigberg, Eichberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and natural-wine tastings at the Eguisheim winery.
vineyards.description: Eguisheim biodynamic estate run by Jean-Francois Ginglinger, six hectares worked by hand on clay-limestone and sandstone including the Pfersigberg Grand Cru.

vineyards.name: Domaine Emile Beyer
vineyards.address: 7 Place du Chateau Saint-Leon, 68420 Eguisheim, France
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Eichberg, Pfersigberg, Hohrain)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Eguisheim winery on Place du Chateau Saint-Leon.
vineyards.description: Eguisheim family domaine since 1580, fourteen generations in the village square at the foot of the Saint-Leon castle, now run biodynamically by Christian and Valerie Beyer.

vineyards.name: Domaine Bruno Sorg
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Pfersigberg, Eichberg, Florimont)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Eguisheim winery.
vineyards.description: Eguisheim family estate established in 1965, working three Grands Crus including Florimont with one of the better Muscat addresses south of Colmar.

vineyards.name: Domaine Agape
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Rosacker, Osterberg, Schoenenbourg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Riquewihr winery; rural rentals on site.
vineyards.description: Riquewihr-based domaine founded by Vincent Sipp in 2007 from a partition of family vineyards, twelve hectares spread across Hunawihr, Ribeauville, Zellenberg and Bergheim Grands Crus.

vineyards.name: Domaine Vincent Stoeffler
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Kirchberg de Barr, Muenchberg) and Klevener de Heiligenstein
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Barr winery.
vineyards.description: Barr family domaine with sixteen hectares between Barr, Mittelbergheim and Heiligenstein, organic for two decades and one of the few non-local Klevener de Heiligenstein bottlers.

vineyards.name: Domaine Loew
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC (lieux-dits Bruderbach, Muschelkalk, Altenberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits by appointment at the Westhoffen winery.
vineyards.description: Westhoffen domaine in the far north of the Route des Vins, certified Demeter and Biodyvin, producing only around 3500 cases per year from limestone lieux-dits.

vineyards.name: Domaine Achillee
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC (granite lieux-dits of Scherwiller)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits at the straw-bale bioclimatic cellar in Scherwiller; tours by booking.
vineyards.description: Scherwiller estate built around a distinctive straw-bale wine cellar, certified organic and Demeter biodynamic with a focus on indigenous yeast fermentations and minimal sulphur.

vineyards.name: Domaine Jean-Marie Haag
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Zinnkoepfle)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits by appointment at the Soultzmatt winery.
vineyards.description: Soultzmatt family domaine under Terra Vitis and HVE sustainable farming, anchor address for the dry Zinnkoepfle Grand Cru above the Vallee Noble.

vineyards.name: Domaine Rietsch
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Zotzenberg) and natural-wine cuvees
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits by appointment at the Mittelbergheim winery; natural-wine focus.
vineyards.description: Mittelbergheim family domaine, certified organic and a touchstone of the Alsace natural-wine current with skin-contact and zero-sulphur cuvees alongside Zotzenberg Sylvaner.

vineyards.name: Domaine Meyer-Fonne
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Wineck-Schlossberg, Sporen, Furstentum, Kaefferkopf, Kitterle)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Katzenthal winery; appointments preferred.
vineyards.description: Katzenthal family domaine since 1732, run by Felix Meyer with fifteen hectares of granite-and-limestone Grands Crus including the Wineck-Schlossberg behind the village.

vineyards.name: Domaine Bernhard-Reibel
vineyards.address: 46 Vieux Chemin de Selestat, 67730 Chatenois, France
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC (Chatenois granite lieux-dits)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits at the Chatenois winery.
vineyards.description: Family domaine in Chatenois at the foot of the Hahnenberg, certified organic, working granite slopes between Selestat and the Vosges with a long Riesling vocation.

vineyards.name: Domaine Melanie Pfister
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Engelberg)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Dahlenheim winery; appointments preferred.
vineyards.description: Dahlenheim domaine run by eighth-generation Melanie Pfister, certified organic since 2021 with biodynamic preparations and access to the Engelberg Grand Cru.

vineyards.name: Domaine Beck-Hartweg
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Frankstein)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment in central Dambach-la-Ville.
vineyards.description: Sixteen-generation Dambach-la-Ville domaine traceable to 1510, organic since 2011 and biodynamic, working the pink-granite Frankstein Grand Cru above the medieval village.

vineyards.name: Domaine Jean Becker
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Schoenenbourg, Froehn)
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Zellenberg winery.
vineyards.description: Zellenberg family domaine since 1610, organic and biodynamic, with parcels on the Froehn Grand Cru, a touchstone for dry Alsace Muscat from clay-marl soils.

vineyards.name: Maison Cattin
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Hatschbourg, Goldert) and Cremant d'Alsace
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Voegtlinshoffen estate.
vineyards.description: Thirteen-generation Voegtlinshoffen estate spanning 90 hectares including parcels on the Hatschbourg and Goldert Grands Crus and a major Cremant programme.

vineyards.name: Domaine Frederic Mochel
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Altenberg de Bergbieten)
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Traenheim winery; appointments preferred.
vineyards.description: Fourteen-generation Traenheim domaine working the Altenberg de Bergbieten Grand Cru and certified organic across its 10-hectare holding.

vineyards.name: Domaine Romain et Jeremie Fritsch
vineyards.address: 49 Rue du General de Gaulle, 67520 Marlenheim, France
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC (Marlenheim lieux-dits Stephansberg, Steinklotz)
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Marlenheim winery.
vineyards.description: Marlenheim father-and-son domaine at the northern gate of the Route des Vins, certified organic, with a focus on the Steinklotz lieu-dit and Marlenheim's red-wine tradition.

verified.address_quoted: 49 rue du General de Gaulle, 67520 Marlenheim

vineyards.name: Domaine Saint-Remy
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Steingrubler, Hengst)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Wettolsheim winery.
vineyards.description: Wettolsheim Ehrhart family domaine biodynamic and organic, with parcels on Steingrubler and Hengst and a strong Pinot Noir vocation on the Herrenweg lieu-dit.

vineyards.name: Cave du Roi Dagobert
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Altenberg de Bergbieten, Steinklotz, Engelberg) and Cremant d'Alsace
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Traenheim cooperative boutique.
vineyards.description: Bas-Rhin cooperative founded in 1952 in Traenheim, with 250 grower members across 1000 hectares spanning the northern half of the Route des Vins.

vineyards.name: Domaine Sick-Dreyer
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Kaefferkopf)
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Ammerschwihr winery.
vineyards.description: Ammerschwihr family domaine with a winemaking legacy traced to 1563, more than two hectares on the Kaefferkopf Grand Cru, an early Alsace site to receive village-level recognition.

vineyards.name: Domaine Pierre Adam
vineyards.address: 8 Rue du Lt Louis Mourier, 68770 Ammerschwihr, France
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Kaefferkopf)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Ammerschwihr winery.
vineyards.description: Four-generation Ammerschwihr domaine, certified organic, with Kaefferkopf and Letzenberg as its terroir signatures and a steady Pinot Noir line.

verified.address_quoted: 8 Rue du Lt Louis Mourier, 68770 Ammerschwihr

vineyards.name: Caveau Lorentz-Klipfel
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC, Alsace Grand Cru (Kirchberg de Barr, Kastelberg, Wiebelsberg, Zotzenberg) and Klevener de Heiligenstein
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Barr boutique; closed Sundays and Mondays.
vineyards.description: Historic Barr house, founded in 1824, with Grand Cru holdings on Kirchberg de Barr, Kastelberg, Wiebelsberg and Zotzenberg, and one of the few outside the five-village pocket to bottle Klevener de Heiligenstein.

vineyards.name: Domaine Martin Schaetzel - Kirrenbourg
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC and Alsace Grand Cru (Kaefferkopf, Rangen de Thann, Schlossberg, Mackrain)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings at the Kientzheim winery; appointments preferred.
vineyards.description: Kientzheim-based estate (formerly Ammerschwihr) certified Demeter biodynamic since the 1990s, with parcels in four Grands Crus including a small Rangen de Thann holding.

vineyards.name: Domaine Hubert Metz
vineyards.classification: Alsace AOC (Winzenberg granite lieux-dits)
vineyards.tasting_program: Daily cellar visits and tastings at the Blienschwiller winery.
vineyards.description: Blienschwiller family domaine certified organic, working the granite Winzenberg slope at the southern end of the Dambach-la-Ville cluster.
